body:
## Summary
- Add `orchestrate` as an explicit route for mention and label triggers:
  - `@sepo-agent /orchestrate`
  - `agent/orchestrate`
- Route explicit orchestrate requests through `agent-router.yml` to `agent-orchestrator.yml`.
- Extend orchestrator dispatch/runtime to support manual orchestrate starts with situation-aware selection:
  - issue target -> `implement`
  - pull request target with `CHANGES_REQUESTED` -> `fix-pr`
  - other open pull request targets -> `review`
- Remove automatic post-action orchestrator dispatch from:
  - `agent-implement.yml`
  - `agent-review.yml`
  - `agent-fix-pr.yml`
- Update docs and test expectations to match the new explicit orchestration behavior.

## Verification
- `npm --prefix .agent run build`
- `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/triage.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js`

Closes #19

comments.body:
I now have complete per-file patches from the live PR and enough evidence to score rubric compliance. I’m drafting the rubric table with one potential warning around failure-path regression coverage in the new manual-orchestrate runtime logic.## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 42/45 (93) | PARTIAL | 6 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / specific | Default follow-up orchestration to one-shot | pass | 8/8 | PR removes automatic orchestrator handoff from implement/review/fix-pr workflows and makes orchestration explicit via route/label (`/orchestrate`, `agent/orchestrate`) in router + triage ([agent-router.yml](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/3eb1b3f5b350b095d7a9bf67248403cec5b37018/.github/workflows/agent-router.yml), [triage.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/3eb1b3f5b350b095d7a9bf67248403cec5b37018/.agent/src/triage.ts), [agent-implement.yml](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/3eb1b3f5b350b095d7a9bf67248403cec5b37018/.github/workflows/agent-implement.yml), [agent-review.yml](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/3eb1b3f5b350b095d7a9bf67248403cec5b37018/.github/workflows/agent-review.yml), [agent-fix-pr.yml](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/3eb1b3f5b350b095d7a9bf67248403cec5b37018/.github/workflows/agent-fix-pr.yml)). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Manual-start logic is added inside existing orchestrator CLI flow and still reuses existing `decideHandoff` path for non-manual starts; dispatch remains through existing helper interfaces ([orchestrate-handoff.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/3eb1b3f5b350b095d7a9bf67248403cec5b37018/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts), [dispatch-agent-orchestrator.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/3eb1b3f5b350b095d7a9bf67248403cec5b37018/.agent/src/cli/dispatch-agent-orchestrator.ts)). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Changes are tightly scoped to routing/orchestration behavior, related runtime CLIs, and matching tests/docs; no unrelated refactor patterns in diff. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | partial | 4/7 | Runtime now stops with explicit reasons when PR status cannot be read or PR is not open (`could not read pull request status`, `pull request is ...`) in [orchestrate-handoff.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/3eb1b3f5b350b095d7a9bf67248403cec5b37018/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts). But added tests focus on routing/envelope wiring; no focused runtime regression test covers those failure branches. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Docs were updated across user-facing and technical pages to match explicit orchestration routing and behavior ([README.md](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/3eb1b3f5b350b095d7a9bf67248403cec5b37018/README.md), [AGENT.md](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/3eb1b3f5b350b095d7a9bf67248403cec5b37018/AGENT.md), [.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/3eb1b3f5b350b095d7a9bf67248403cec5b37018/.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md), [.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/3eb1b3f5b350b095d7a9bf67248403cec5b37018/.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md)). |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | Manual orchestrate selection is straightforward and deterministic (issue→implement, PR+changes requested→fix-pr, otherwise review), with minimal added complexity ([orchestrate-handoff.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/3eb1b3f5b350b095d7a9bf67248403cec5b37018/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts), [orchestrator-preflight.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/3eb1b3f5b350b095d7a9bf67248403cec5b37018/.agent/src/cli/orchestrator-preflight.ts)). |

## Notes

- Biggest positive driver: the PR directly enforces explicit orchestration intent and removes implicit follow-up chaining, aligning with the one-shot follow-up rubric.
- Smallest useful follow-up: add a focused test for manual orchestrate failure paths in runtime logic (PR status read failure / closed PR) to fully satisfy stateful-failure coverage expectations.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** `surface-stateful-failures` is only partially met. Failure reasons are surfaced in runtime decisions, but there is no targeted regression test for new manual-orchestrate stop branches in `orchestrate-handoff` behavior.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PARTIAL

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- This PR implements issue #19 by making orchestration an explicit top-level route (`/orchestrate` and `agent/orchestrate`) instead of an implicit post-action follow-up.
- It updates router/triage to recognize the new route, dispatches `agent-orchestrator.yml` explicitly, and removes automatic orchestrator handoff steps from `implement`, `review`, and `fix-pr` workflows.
- It also adds manual-start orchestration logic so issue targets dispatch `implement`, PR targets with `CHANGES_REQUESTED` dispatch `fix-pr`, and other open PRs dispatch `review`.
- Docs and tests were updated to reflect this explicit orchestration model and the new routing behavior.
- This aligns with the parent issue goal (reduce implicit automation surprises and make orchestration user-invoked).

## Review
- The direction is strong and largely well-scoped, but there is one blocking authorization gap plus a few correctness/clarity follow-ups around docs and test depth.
- Inline comments were posted for the blocking delegated-policy issue and one concrete workflow annotation typo.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Delegated route authorization bypass in manual `/orchestrate` dispatch | BLOCKING | `orchestrate` authorization is checked at route entry, but downstream `implement`/`fix-pr`/`review` dispatches do not re-validate per-route policy, allowing stricter route overrides to be bypassed. |
| Planner behavior docs do not match explicit orchestrate runtime | WARNING | Docs state `agent` mode “can run the planner route first,” but preflight currently disables planner when `source_action=orchestrate`, making explicit starts deterministic-only. |
| Manual orchestrate decision/failure paths lack focused behavior tests | WARNING | New stop/dispatch branches in `orchestrate-handoff` (PR status read failure, closed PR, budget exhausted, unsupported target kind) are not covered by direct behavioral tests. |
| Misleading router section header for orchestrate job | INFO | The `orchestrate` job is labeled as `Route: review`, which is a copy/paste clarity defect. |

## Progress
- Explicit orchestration route and removal of implicit auto-handoff are implemented and match issue #19 intent.
- Rubrics review already acknowledged missing focused regression coverage for manual orchestrate failure branches.
- Inline feedback has already been posted for:
  - delegated-route auth gap in `.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts` (line 326)
  - copied section header in `.github/workflows/agent-router.yml` (line 626)

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Delegated route authorization bypass in manual `/orchestrate` dispatch</summary>

**Cause:**  
Manual `/orchestrate` is policy-checked as `orchestrate` in routing, then dispatches downstream actions directly in handoff runtime without re-checking downstream route authorization.

**Candidate solutions:**  
- Re-check effective access policy for `decision.nextAction` before dispatching in `orchestrate-handoff`.
- Or enforce a strict invariant that `orchestrate` permissions must be the intersection of downstream routes and validate this at policy parse/startup.

**Comments:**  
One review considered this the main rework trigger; other reviews focused on docs/tests and did not dispute the behavior.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Planner behavior docs do not match explicit orchestrate runtime</summary>

**Cause:**  
`plannerEnabled` is gated by `sourceAction !== "orchestrate"` in preflight, while docs for supported workflows describe planner-first behavior for `agent` mode in general terms.

**Candidate solutions:**  
- Update docs to explicitly state: manual `/orchestrate` starts are deterministic-only today.
- Or change preflight/runtime to allow planner on explicit orchestrate starts and document constraints.

**Comments:**  
This is a doc/runtime contract mismatch rather than a runtime crash.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Manual orchestrate decision/failure paths lack focused behavior tests</summary>

**Cause:**  
Current additions mostly verify wiring/text patterns; there are no direct behavior tests for `decideManualOrchestration` stop paths and branch selection edge cases.

**Candidate solutions:**  
- Add targeted tests for: round-budget exhaustion, PR status read failure, non-open PR, unsupported target kind, and each dispatch branch.
- Keep envelope tests for wiring but add runtime-level assertions on decision outputs.

**Comments:**  
This point is consistent across Codex/Claude/rubrics inputs.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Misleading router section header for orchestrate job</summary>

**Cause:**  
Copied comment label says `Route: review` immediately above the `orchestrate` job.

**Candidate solutions:**  
- Rename header to `# --- Route: orchestrate ---`.

**Comments:**  
Low risk but worth fixing for maintainability.
</details>

## Recommended Next Step
- `FIX_PR`: unresolved findings include a concrete blocking policy bug plus straightforward doc/test updates that are safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
- `NEEDS_REWORK`

## Action Items
- [ ] Enforce downstream route authorization before `orchestrate-handoff` dispatches `implement`/`review`/`fix-pr` (or enforce and validate strict policy inheritance rules).
- [ ] Add focused behavior tests for manual orchestrate decision and stop branches in `orchestrate-handoff`.
- [ ] Reconcile docs with runtime planner behavior for explicit `/orchestrate` starts (or change runtime to match docs).
- [ ] Fix the copied router header above the `orchestrate` job (`Route: orchestrate`).

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 2/5).

agent planner selected fix-pr: Latest review synthesis on PR #20 recommends `FIX_PR` and reports unresolved concrete findings, including a blocking delegated route-authorization gap; round budget remains (1/5).

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777398628588 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUwNjg0NTA4MDI6cmV2aWV3OjIwOmZpeF9wcjoyMDoy -->

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `agent/implement-issue-19/codex-25067966881`. Requested by @lolipopshock.

- Enforced delegated-route authorization for explicit `/orchestrate` handoffs in `.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts` before dispatching `implement`/`review`/`fix-pr`.
- Wired authorization context through orchestration dispatch path:
  - `.github/workflows/agent-router.yml` now forwards `AUTHOR_ASSOCIATION`, `ACCESS_POLICY`, and `REPOSITORY_PRIVATE`.
  - `.agent/src/cli/dispatch-agent-orchestrator.ts` forwards those fields to `agent-orchestrator.yml`.
  - `.github/workflows/agent-orchestrator.yml` accepts/forwards these inputs to `orchestrate-handoff`.
- Added focused manual-orchestrate behavior coverage in `.agent/src/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.ts` for:
  - round-budget exhaustion
  - unsupported target kind
  - PR status read failure
  - non-open PR stop
  - branch selection (`issue -> implement`, `PR+CHANGES_REQUESTED -> fix-pr`, other open `PR -> review`)
  - delegated auth re-validation stop path
- Reconciled docs with runtime behavior for explicit orchestrate starts (deterministic-only) in:
  - `.agent/docs/actions/agent-actions.md`
  - `.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md`
  - `.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md`
- Fixed copied section header in `.github/workflows/agent-router.yml` to `# --- Route: orchestrate ---`.

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

- `npm --prefix .agent run build`
- `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/triage.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.js`

</details>

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 8 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / specific | Default follow-up orchestration to one-shot | pass | 8/8 | Automatic post-action orchestrator handoff was removed from [`agent-implement.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/57eee1f086e8a4bfa590fd4a0cf7b1fa30848109/.github/workflows/agent-implement.yml), [`agent-review.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/57eee1f086e8a4bfa590fd4a0cf7b1fa30848109/.github/workflows/agent-review.yml), and [`agent-fix-pr.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/57eee1f086e8a4bfa590fd4a0cf7b1fa30848109/.github/workflows/agent-fix-pr.yml); explicit `/orchestrate` route/label added in [`triage.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/57eee1f086e8a4bfa590fd4a0cf7b1fa30848109/.agent/src/triage.ts) and [`agent-router.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/57eee1f086e8a4bfa590fd4a0cf7b1fa30848109/.github/workflows/agent-router.yml). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Manual orchestration logic was added in existing orchestrator runtime, reusing existing policy/helpers (`parseAccessPolicy`, route association checks, dispatch path) in [`orchestrate-handoff.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/57eee1f086e8a4bfa590fd4a0cf7b1fa30848109/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts), not duplicated in workflow YAML. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Changes are scoped to routing/orchestrator behavior, targeted tests, and matching docs from the issue scope (`Closes #19`), with no unrelated refactor sweep in the PR file list. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | pass | 7/7 | Runtime now explicitly stops with concrete reasons for PR-state read failure/closed PR/unsupported targets in [`orchestrate-handoff.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/57eee1f086e8a4bfa590fd4a0cf7b1fa30848109/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts), and focused regression coverage exists in [`orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/57eee1f086e8a4bfa590fd4a0cf7b1fa30848109/.agent/src/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.ts). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | The implementation aligns directly to linked issue intent in [#19](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/19) (explicit orchestrate route + situation-aware start), and the PR body maps exactly to that context. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | User-facing and technical docs were updated with the behavior shift, including [`README.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/57eee1f086e8a4bfa590fd4a0cf7b1fa30848109/README.md), [`AGENT.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/57eee1f086e8a4bfa590fd4a0cf7b1fa30848109/AGENT.md), [`agent-actions.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/57eee1f086e8a4bfa590fd4a0cf7b1fa30848109/.agent/docs/actions/agent-actions.md), and [`agent-orchestrator.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/57eee1f086e8a4bfa590fd4a0cf7b1fa30848109/.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md). |
| coding_workflow / generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 6/6 | Docs describe behavior and workflow structure without introducing per-file inventory tables; updates stay principle-oriented. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | Manual start selection remains simple and deterministic (`issue -> implement`, `PR + CHANGES_REQUESTED -> fix-pr`, else `review`) in [`orchestrate-handoff.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/57eee1f086e8a4bfa590fd4a0cf7b1fa30848109/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts) with minimal new control surface. |

## Notes

- Strongest score drivers were explicit one-shot orchestration behavior, focused runtime failure surfacing, and synchronized docs/tests.
- Not scored as applicable to this PR: `default-merge-strategy` (no merge action in this PR), `abstract-learned-rubrics` (rubrics-maintenance route only).

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific blocking or warning findings for the selected applicable rubrics.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- This PR implements issue #19 by making orchestration explicit instead of implicit: users invoke it with `@sepo-agent /orchestrate` or `agent/orchestrate`.
- Routing now recognizes the new route and dispatches `agent-orchestrator.yml` directly, while automatic post-action orchestrator chaining was removed from implement/review/fix-pr workflows.
- Manual orchestrate starts are now situation-aware: issue targets route to implement, PRs with `CHANGES_REQUESTED` route to fix-pr, and other open PRs route to review.
- The PR also adds focused runtime tests for manual orchestrate decisions and updates docs to match the explicit orchestration model.
- Earlier blocking findings (delegated authorization gap, docs mismatch, missing branch tests, copied header) were addressed in later commits.

## Review
Overall quality is strong and aligned with the issue goal; most prior risks appear resolved on current head.  
One reviewer still identified a concrete UX warning (silent stop path for explicit `/orchestrate`), while other reviewer inputs reported no remaining defects.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Explicit `/orchestrate` stop paths can appear as silent no-ops | WARNING | `orchestrate-handoff` can decide `stop`, but the workflow does not post a thread-visible status message, so users may see route acceptance without clear outcome feedback. |

## Progress
- Delegated downstream route authorization was fixed and covered by tests.
- Manual orchestrate failure/branch behavior coverage was added in `orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.ts`.
- Planner-behavior docs were reconciled with runtime behavior for explicit `/orchestrate`.
- Router header typo was fixed.
- I posted one new inline comment for the remaining warning: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/20#discussion_r3158403206

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Explicit `/orchestrate` stop paths can appear as silent no-ops</summary>

**Cause:**  
The orchestrator workflow dispatch step can end in `stop` without a follow-up PR/issue comment, even though the request was explicitly user-triggered and previously acknowledged.

**Candidate solutions:**  
- Add a conditional post-response step in `agent-orchestrator.yml` when `steps.handoff.outputs.decision == 'stop'` to publish a short reason (budget exhausted, PR closed, delegated auth denied, etc.).
- Reuse existing response-posting utilities for consistency with other route outcomes.

**Comments:**  
Claude flagged this as a warning; Codex and rubric review reported no remaining defects, so this is a meaningful reviewer disagreement on UX strictness rather than core correctness.
</details>

## Recommended Next Step
`FIX_PR`: there is one concrete, low-risk workflow improvement remaining, and it is safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
`MINOR_ISSUES`

## Action Items
- [ ] Add a stop-outcome status comment path in `agent-orchestrator.yml` for explicit `/orchestrate` runs so accepted requests do not fail silently.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 2/5).

agent planner selected fix-pr: Latest review synthesis for PR #20 (MINOR_ISSUES) explicitly recommends FIX_PR, and one concrete unresolved workflow UX issue remains that is safe to automate within round budget.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777432854284 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUwODg5NDg0MDM6cmV2aWV3OjIwOmZpeF9wcjoyMDoy -->

**Sepo did not produce code changes for this PR.**

Please add more context or restate the requested fixes, then try again.

Implemented and validated on the current PR branch.

What’s in place:
- Added explicit orchestrate routing:
  - Mention route: `@sepo-agent /orchestrate`
  - Label route: `agent/orchestrate`
- Routed explicit orchestrate requests from router to orchestrator workflow.
- Extended manual orchestrate runtime selection:
  - issue target -> `implement`
  - PR with `CHANGES_REQUESTED` -> `fix-pr`
  - other open PR -> `review`
- Removed automatic post-action orchestrator handoff from:
  - `agent-implement.yml`
  - `agent-review.yml`
  - `agent-fix-pr.yml`
- Updated docs/tests to reflect explicit orchestration behavior.
- Fixed router header typo to `Route: orchestrate`.

Verification run:
- `npm --prefix .agent run build` ✅
- `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/triage.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js` ✅

Point-by-point:

1. **Removing post-review handoff does not remove orchestration entirely.**  
   It removes implicit post-action chaining from `agent-review.yml`. Orchestration still works through the explicit route added in [`agent-router.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/57eee1f086e8a4bfa590fd4a0cf7b1fa30848109/.github/workflows/agent-router.yml#L626-L671), which dispatches `agent-orchestrator.yml` for `/orchestrate` or `agent/orchestrate`.

2. **I agree with adding a visible stop response.**  
   Today `orchestrate-handoff` emits `decision=stop` and `reason`, but [`agent-orchestrator.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/57eee1f086e8a4bfa590fd4a0cf7b1fa30848109/.github/workflows/agent-orchestrator.yml#L159-L180) does not post that outcome back. Follow-up fix: add a step after `Decide and dispatch next action` gated on `steps.handoff.outputs.decision == 'stop'` and `inputs.source_action == 'orchestrate'`, then post a short comment with the reason.

3. **I agree the access model should be stricter at route entry.**  
   The current fix re-validates delegated access just before dispatch in [`orchestrate-handoff.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/57eee1f086e8a4bfa590fd4a0cf7b1fa30848109/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L228-L251), which prevents bypasses. But your point is stronger: `/orchestrate` should probably not be accepted unless the requester has sufficient access for the downstream routes it may invoke. Follow-up fix: enforce this during router authorization:
   - issue `/orchestrate` requires `implement` access
   - PR `/orchestrate` requires `review` and `fix-pr` access, or at least the route selected from current PR state
   - return an unsupported/denied response immediately instead of accepting and later stopping

4. **Given no backward compatibility requirement, we can simplify further.**  
   Since issue #19 is explicitly moving away from automatic handoff, we should remove or narrow remaining action-originated orchestration compatibility. Follow-up fix: keep `dispatch-agent-orchestrator.ts` only as the explicit route dispatcher, or rename/simplify it so it no longer carries post-action handoff semantics.

Recommended next fixes:
- Add stop-outcome comment for explicit `/orchestrate`.
- Move downstream route access validation into router-time `/orchestrate` authorization.
- Simplify docs/code names that still describe “post-action handoff” behavior.

@sepo-agent /answer

> Removing post-review handoff does not remove orchestration entirely.
It removes implicit post-action chaining from agent-review.yml. Orchestration still works through the explicit route added in agent-router.yml, which dispatches agent-orchestrator.yml for /orchestrate or agent/orchestrate

But this is incorrect? After the implement and fix route, the orchestrate agent cannot be called again if you remove that lines? Meaning that the orchestrate is just one off

You’re right. My previous wording was too loose.

With the current PR shape:

- `/orchestrate` can still be invoked again manually by the user.
- But once `/orchestrate` dispatches `implement`, `review`, or `fix-pr`, that downstream action will **not hand back to the orchestrator** because the post-action handoff steps were removed.
- So yes: the current implementation makes orchestrate a **one-shot router**, not an ongoing orchestration mode.

That means if the intended UX is:

`/orchestrate` -> implement -> review -> fix-pr -> review -> stop

then we should not remove handoff entirely. We should remove only the implicit handoff for normal `/implement`, `/review`, and `/fix-pr` runs.

Recommended fix:

1. Add an explicit orchestration context/input, e.g. `orchestration_enabled: true` or `orchestration_run_id`.
2. When `/orchestrate` dispatches a sub-action, pass that input into `agent-implement.yml`, `agent-review.yml`, and `agent-fix-pr.yml`.
3. Restore the post-action handoff steps, but gate them on that explicit orchestration context only.
4. Normal `/review`, `/implement`, `/fix-pr` stay one-shot.
5. Explicit `/orchestrate` becomes the bounded multi-step mode.

So the better model is:

- Direct route: `/review` -> review only.
- Explicit orchestrator route: `/orchestrate` -> selected action -> orchestrator handoff loop until stop condition.

That matches the original UX concern while preserving real orchestration.

> You’re right. My previous wording was too loose.
> 
> With the current PR shape:
> 
> - `/orchestrate` can still be invoked again manually by the user.
> - But once `/orchestrate` dispatches `implement`, `review`, or `fix-pr`, that downstream action will **not hand back to the orchestrator** because the post-action handoff steps were removed.
> - So yes: the current implementation makes orchestrate a **one-shot router**, not an ongoing orchestration mode.
> 
> That means if the intended UX is:
> 
> `/orchestrate` -> implement -> review -> fix-pr -> review -> stop
> 
> then we should not remove handoff entirely. We should remove only the implicit handoff for normal `/implement`, `/review`, and `/fix-pr` runs.
> 
> Recommended fix:
> 
> 1. Add an explicit orchestration context/input, e.g. `orchestration_enabled: true` or `orchestration_run_id`.
> 2. When `/orchestrate` dispatches a sub-action, pass that input into `agent-implement.yml`, `agent-review.yml`, and `agent-fix-pr.yml`.
> 3. Restore the post-action handoff steps, but gate them on that explicit orchestration context only.
> 4. Normal `/review`, `/implement`, `/fix-pr` stay one-shot.
> 5. Explicit `/orchestrate` becomes the bounded multi-step mode.
> 
> So the better model is:
> 
> - Direct route: `/review` -> review only.
> - Explicit orchestrator route: `/orchestrate` -> selected action -> orchestrator handoff loop until stop condition.
> 
> That matches the original UX concern while preserving real orchestration.

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- This PR implements issue #19 by making orchestration an explicit route via `@sepo-agent /orchestrate` and the `agent/orchestrate` label.
- The router now dispatches `agent-orchestrator.yml`, which selects `implement`, `review`, or `fix-pr` based on the current issue/PR state.
- Direct `/implement`, `/review`, and `/fix-pr` requests remain one-shot, while `/orchestrate` carries explicit orchestration context so bounded follow-up handoffs can continue.
- The PR updates docs and tests for explicit orchestration, access-policy checks, manual decision paths, and the clarified multi-step orchestration behavior.
- Latest `main` was merged into the PR branch cleanly before this review pass.

## Review
The PR is close, but one reviewer found a remaining authorization gap in continuation handoffs after the first `/orchestrate` dispatch. The rubric review passes current head, so the meaningful disagreement is whether first-hop delegated authorization is sufficient for the full explicit chain; the concrete code review shows it is not.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Orchestrated continuation handoffs bypass downstream route authorization | BLOCKING | Delegated route authorization only runs when `SOURCE_ACTION=orchestrate`; later `implement`/`review`/`fix-pr` handoffs in the same explicit chain can dispatch routes without checking the original requester against route overrides. |

## Progress
- Earlier delegated authorization feedback for the initial `/orchestrate` dispatch was addressed.
- Focused manual orchestration tests were added for first-hop branch selection and stop paths.
- Docs now describe direct routes as one-shot and `/orchestrate` as bounded explicit orchestration.
- Prior concerns about one-shot orchestration semantics were acknowledged and fixed by restoring gated handoffs through `orchestration_enabled`.
- A new inline comment was posted for the remaining continuation authorization issue: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/20#discussion_r3163163994

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Orchestrated continuation handoffs bypass downstream route authorization</summary>

**Cause:**  
`applyDelegatedRouteAuthorization()` only enforces delegated route access when `sourceAction` normalizes to `orchestrate`. After the first dispatch, action workflows hand back with `SOURCE_ACTION=implement`, `review`, or `fix-pr`, so the next `decision.nextAction` can bypass `AGENT_ACCESS_POLICY.route_overrides`.

**Candidate solutions:**  
- Preserve original `author_association`, `access_policy`, and repository visibility through orchestrated action workflows, then validate every `decision.nextAction` before dispatch.
- Or authorize the full possible orchestration chain up front before starting `/orchestrate`.
- Add regression coverage for continuation denial, such as `SOURCE_ACTION=review`, `ORCHESTRATION_ENABLED=true`, a review decision requiring `fix-pr`, and a `fix-pr` route override denying the requester.

**Comments:**  
Rubric review reports PASS on current head, but the code review identifies a concrete policy bypass not covered by existing tests, so this should block shipping.
</details>

## Recommended Next Step
`FIX_PR`: the remaining issue is a concrete authorization bug with a clear branch-change path and focused test coverage to add.

## Final Verdict
`NEEDS_REWORK`

## Action Items
- [ ] Enforce downstream route authorization for every orchestrated continuation handoff, not only the initial `SOURCE_ACTION=orchestrate` dispatch.
- [ ] Preserve the original requester authorization context through `agent-implement.yml`, `agent-review.yml`, and `agent-fix-pr.yml` when `orchestration_enabled` is true.
- [ ] Add a regression test covering a denied continuation route, such as review handoff to `fix-pr` when `route_overrides.fix-pr` denies the requester.

@sepo-agent /answer 

overall looks fine; but what does this do now? since we should only support orchestrator based hand-off ? 

> Automation is disabled by default. Configure `AGENT_AUTOMATION_MODE` as a mode value:

| Mode | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `disabled` | Default for action workflows. Orchestrator still runs when users explicitly request `/orchestrate`. |Collapse comment

That wording is now confusing, and I think it should be changed.

Historically `AGENT_AUTOMATION_MODE` meant: “should normal action workflows automatically hand off to the orchestrator after they finish?” In the new design, that should no longer be the control point. Normal `/review`, `/implement`, and `/fix-pr` should stay one-shot.

If we only support **orchestrator-based handoff**, then the handoff should be controlled by explicit orchestration context, not by a global “automation enabled” flag.

Better model:

- `/review` -> review only
- `/implement` -> implement only
- `/fix-pr` -> fix only
- `/orchestrate` -> orchestrator starts a bounded loop
- downstream handoff happens only when the downstream action was launched by `/orchestrate`

So this doc section should not say:

```md
Automation is disabled by default. Configure `AGENT_AUTOMATION_MODE`...
```

Recommended follow-up change:

```md
The orchestrator is an explicit route. Use `/orchestrate` or `agent/orchestrate`
to start a bounded orchestration run. Normal action routes do not hand off after
completion.

`AGENT_AUTOMATION_MODE` only selects the orchestrator decision strategy for an
explicit orchestration run:
```

And the table should be something like:

| Mode | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `heuristics` | Deterministic built-in state machine. |
| `agent` | Planner-assisted orchestration, validated by runtime policy. |

I would remove `disabled` unless we specifically want a config that makes `/orchestrate` unavailable. Right now “disabled but `/orchestrate` still runs” is internally contradictory.

> And the table should be something like:

Mode	Meaning
heuristics	Deterministic built-in state machine.
agent	Planner-assisted orchestration, validated by runtime policy.
I would remove disabled unless we specifically want a config that makes /orchestrate unavailable. Right now “disabled but /orchestrate still runs” is internally contradictory.

Yes this makes more sense. 

@sepo-agent /fix-pr can you do a small change to fix it?

- `.agent/docs/customization/configuration-list.md`
  - `AGENT_AUTOMATION_MODE` now documents only `heuristics` and `agent` as active decision modes.
  - `AGENT_AUTOMATION_MAX_ROUNDS` now describes explicit orchestration handoff rounds.

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Orchestrated continuation handoffs bypass downstream route authorization</summary>

**Cause:**  
`applyDelegatedRouteAuthorization()` returns early unless `sourceAction` is `orchestrate`. After `/orchestrate` dispatches the first action, later handoffs return with `SOURCE_ACTION=implement`, `review`, or `fix-pr`, and the action workflows do not preserve `AUTHOR_ASSOCIATION`, `ACCESS_POLICY`, or `REPOSITORY_PRIVATE`.

reviews.body: @sepo-agent can you take a look my comments and provide point by point feedback to address this and potential follow up fixe

title: Make orchestrator explicit via /orchestrate and label route
